merged_findings: Strong linear dependencies and perfect or near-perfect correlations exist among top-ranked feature pairs such as 'Bwd Packet Length Mean' and 'Avg Bwd Segment Size', and 'Fwd Header Length' and 'Fwd Header Length.1', indicating significant redundancy.
These redundant features maintain moderate shortcut-like predictive leverage, contributing spurious signals and creating tension between redundancy and their individual top-rank importance.
Local label noise and distributional overlap are observed around redundant feature clusters, further complicating the redundancy versus importance relationship.
The tension between redundancy and top-rank importance remains unresolved and preserved as a grounded local contradiction.
Impact of redundancy removal on model performance is implied but not directly quantified within the bounded local context.
preserved_contradictions: Tension between redundancy and top-rank importance preserved as unresolved local contradiction
Features with perfect correlation pairs are also top-ranked individually, preserving the tension between redundancy and importance
open_gaps: No direct quantification of redundancy removal impact on performance within local evidence
Distributional overlap verification not performed due to budget constraints, limiting full assessment
Shortcut verification limited to subset of redundant features; broader feature set not exhaustively tested
update_focus: Consolidate local evidence on redundancy and importance tension without resolving contradictions or quantifying performance impact
